basics.name: Junwoo Jung
basics.label: Physics Undergraduate
basics.summary: Physics undergraduate at KAIST working on neutral-atom quantum computing and Rydberg-atom platforms.

education.institution: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)

work.name: Quantum Computing Lab (Prof. Jaewook Ahn), KAIST
highlights: Deterministic error mitigation for Rydberg MIS experiments
TTS(r) scaling for analog optimization on QuEra Aquila

work.name: Condensed Matter Theory Group (Prof. Gil Young Cho), KAIST
highlights: Subleading corrections to entanglement area laws and chiral central charge extraction in (2+1)D topological phases

work.name: The inQlings (Prof. Nelly Ng), NTU Singapore
highlights: Quantum thermodynamics and resource theories: Thermal Operations vs. Gibbs-preserving covariant channels

work.name: Complex Systems & Statistical Physics Lab (Prof. Hawoong Jeong), KAIST
highlights: Entanglement theory and catalytic transformations under LOCC-style constraints

work.name: Quantum Matter Lab (Prof. Jae-yoon Choi), KAIST
highlights: Lab monitoring/analysis utilities and Python-based simulations

work.name: Quantum Computing Lab (Prof. Jaewook Ahn), KAIST
highlights: Rydberg-atom graph constructions for QUBO mapping

volunteer.summary: Intensive winter program in theoretical physics at Korea Institute for Advanced Study (KIAS) and Seoul National University.
Joint winter school on quantum science and technology organized by KAIST and MIT.

awards.title: Second Place Presentation Award
awards.summary: Oral presentation: Exploring the Gap between Thermal Operations and Gibbs-Preserving Covariant Channels

publications.name: Deterministically Error-Mitigated Performance in Rydberg Quantum Computing for the Maximum Independent Set Problem

publications.name: Rydberg-atom graphs for quadratic unconstrained binary optimization problems
